comments.content:
[IMAGE:0]Midnight is upon us and so it's time to launch a new Magic Monday. Ask me anything about occultism, and with certain exceptions noted below, any question received by midnight Monday Eastern time will get an answer. Please note:  Any question or comment received after that point will not get an answer, and in fact will just be deleted.  If you're in a hurry, or suspect you may be the 341,928th person to ask a question, please check out the very rough version 1.3 of The Magic Monday FAQ here. 

Also: I will not be putting through or answering any more questions about practicing magic around children. I've answered those in simple declarative sentences in the FAQ. If you read the FAQ and don't think your question has been answered, read it again. If that doesn't help, consider remedial reading classes; yes, it really is as simple and straightforward as the FAQ says.  And further:  I've decided that questions about getting goodies from spirits are also permanently off topic here. The point of occultism is to develop your own capacities, not to try to bully or wheedle other beings into doing things for you. I've discussed this in a post on my blog.

The image? I field a lot of questions about my books these days, so I've decided to do little capsule summaries of them here, one per week.  This is my seventy-seventh published book, a study of the utterly unfashionable approach to sexual energies in magic that Dion Fortune and her fellow occultists of the Society of the Inner Light pioneered. You can always get a lot of interest by telling people to engage in orgiastic excess, and curiously enough, you can also get a lot of interest by telling people that sex is bad and God will spank them for thinking about it. Suggest something in between these extremes and nothing like so many people want to hear about it. Be that as it may, this is a practical handbook of magical practices that use redirected, sublimated erotic energies; the participants sit several feet from each other, usually with an altar in between them, and stay fully clothed. How dowdy? Nonetheless the methods work extremely well, and I give full details. If you're interested, you can get copies here in the US and here elsewhere. 

Buy Me A Coffee

Ko-Fi

I've had several people ask about tipping me for answers here, and though I certainly don't require that I won't turn it down. You can use either of the links above to access my online tip jar; Buymeacoffee is good for small tips, Ko-Fi is better for larger ones. (I used to use PayPal but they developed an allergy to free speech, so I've developed an allergy to them.) If you're interested in political and economic astrology, or simply prefer to use a subscription service to support your favorite authors, you can find my Patreon page here and my SubscribeStar page here. 
 
[IMAGE:1]I've also had quite a few people over the years ask me where they should buy my books, and here's the answer. Bookshop.org is an alternative online bookstore that supports local bookstores and authors, which a certain gargantuan corporation doesn't, and I have a shop there, which you can check out here. Please consider patronizing it if you'd like to purchase any of my books online.

And don't forget to look up your Pangalactic New Age Soul Signature at CosmicOom.com.

With that said, have at it!

***This Magic Monday is now closed, and no further comments will be put through. See you next week!***

comments.title: Odd Electric Pulse
comments.content:
I had an experience earlier in the week that I am trying to understand.

I was dreaming about a certain high-profile American Catholic bishop leading a prayer outside the Vatican. I was drawn into whatever he was doing, which felt like the pulling of countless consciousnesses together while at the same time splitting them apart. I got the sense that despite his best intentions, something went wrong, or he was overly ambitious, or perhaps I was because it was my dream, and I woke up with what felt like an electric shock. Like a literal, very physical finger-in-socket feeling throughout my body.

I couldn't sleep. I looked at my phone, scrolled through the news, only to find out the new pope had been chosen right around the time I felt that shock.

Some background: although I am baptized, I am not a practicing Catholic. My main practice is the Druidry of the AODA and your books, with some Taiwanese folk-religious observances with my wife. I do pray the glorious mysteries of the rosary every Sunday for my departed relatives and ancestors, who were all Catholic. I am also working on the Gradd y Gwyddon y Ffordd of the Dolman Arch. I don't invoke a pantheon during the SOP -- divination has told me to hold off (this was a while ago).

1) Are intensely physical sensations of energy like this to be expected as work continues with the Druid mysteries? Energies I've experienced through Druidry has been very gentle. This includes my years working through the Druidry Magic Handbook. This was on a whole other level.
2) As touched on above, I've been active in a number of traditions. Considering you have practiced many traditions, have you run into issues of energetic disharmony or overload?
3) You have mentioned before the permanent initiatory effect of baptism. Yet I can't imagine this experience being a common one in modern Catholicism. Is it possible that the Western Mystery work of the Druidic tradition can make more sensitive to Catholic currents?
4) I have a nasty chest cold. Can physical illness disrupt the protective qualities of the SOP?
5) My divination on the event was a bit prickly and perplexing:

What do I need to know about waking up with the feeling of an electric shock from the dream the night before last?
Myself: <---,,-- Luis
The dream itself: <--////- Straif
The result: <---''-- Duir

I was wondering if I should continue questioning with more divination, or just let it sit for a while?

Reversed Luis indicates I could just confuse myself further. Meanwhile, reversed Duir indicates I might need some guidance, so I've approached you for some direction. Despite the very Catholic imagery, I hesitate to contact a priest. My sense is that the church isn't very receptive to discussing these sorts of experiences nowadays.

replies.title: Re: Odd Electric Pulse
replies.content:
How very odd.

1) It really varies. I've had a few such experiences, but only a few; most of my experiences with Druidry have been much more gentle.

2) Only when I've combined energetic practices from more than one; no, praying the Rosary doesn't count (though it's a worthwhile practice).

3) It's possible, yes, though I haven't heard of anyone else having the same experience.

4) Not usually.

5) In your place I'd let it sit for a while; there are obstacles to your understanding that you won't get past just now. I would encourage you to observe your spiritual and psychological condition; you may have received an unexpected gift or blessing.

replies.title: Re: Odd Electric Pulse
replies.content:
Hi JMG,

(Not the OP)

1. What did you mean by the permanent initiatory nature of baptism?

a. What are the benefits and downsides to baptism?

b. I am nominally a Catholic, but I don't practice. I do pray in my own way, but that's it. Would the fact that I'm Catholic/baptised be one of the reasons I'm having difficulties trying to explore Irish/Celtic Druidry? 

I would love to explore Irish Druidry but the Catholic guilt is proving to be very difficult. Would you have any suggestions as to how I could proceed?

Many thanks to you.

replies.title: Re: Odd Electric Pulse
replies.content:
1) Every initiation is permanent. By that I mean that the changes it sets in motion cannot be reversed; you have to live with the consequences. Baptism is the Christian form of initiation. As for benefits and downsides, like every initiation, it connects the initiate to the egregor of the tradition and organization, and so the initiate gets to share in both the positive and negative aspects of that egregor.

2) No. My late wife was also baptized into the Catholic church and had no trouble becoming an enthusiastic and active Druid.

3) You have to deal with the emotional programming you received -- that's the source of the guilt you mention. The Catholic church is notorious for using all sorts of dubious methods to make people dependent on their hierarchy. Journaling is one way to get access to the level where that emotional programming functions; some people also have good results from work with a therapist.

replies.title: Re: Odd Electric Pulse
replies.content:
I've had the electric jolt before, and I'm neither Catholic nor Druid. Mine happened to me when I was working in AMORC and very involved in the local Lodge. There were a few nefarious members at the time and I had fallen afoul of one of them, and, without going in to a lot of hewey fooie, I had a dream and that occurred in it.
Well, my opinion is that there's a lot of different kinds of energy floating around out there,and some of it is free flowing and you can just blunder in to it and get jolted. Sometimes it's been directed at you by somebody or something, other times it's just a stray current you're unfortunate enough to trip over. It's usually not a good idea to mix multiple methods of cultivating subtle energy because this can happen, and sometimes you can just plain over-do things. And always, if you ask your subconscious mind what it means, it will oblige you with an answer, true or not. So keep a salt shaker around and take it easy, and if your dream seems like a "Big" dream, write it down with the date and ponder it later, but the analysis part of the process should only happen after the writing it down part.

replies.title: Re: Odd Electric Pulse
replies.content: This is quite strange. I grew up Catholic and have left the church for good reasons; but I had a very strong urge to return to mass, a feeling I know is not coming from within me for the past few days; ever since the new pope was elected, in fact...

replies.title: Re: Odd Electric Pulse
replies.content:
Hello Anonymoose, you're not alone in this.

I walked away from the Catholic church as an adolescent, which was many decades ago, and wouldn't go back if you paid me all the treasures in the Vatican. But your comment prompts me to note that, yes, by the way, it has been popping into my mind fairly often that I should go to mass! And that is quite strange.

It's goes like this: Thought: Hmm, Latin mass, something good there, maybe I should go. Me: Nah. WHUT?!

There's a lot going on in the nonphysical realms right now, I know that much, and in addition, many devout Catholics praying for one thing and 10 others, including that more people "come back into the fold" and attend mass. (Among them many of my own relatives.) I have drawn my personal conclusions on this recurring "thought-to-attend-mass," however, I don't want to offend any believers who may be here. Not too tough to guess.

That the Latin mass may have some power, that I do not dispute. My beef is with the Church.

What prompted me to respond to your comment here is that, over on the covid forum, several people said that in 2021 they had thoughts, even a woman's voice pop into their minds that said, "get vaccinated / it's easy / get it over with/ just do it." I was not one of them. But I don't have reason to question that it happened.

replies.title: Re: Odd Electric Pulse
replies.content: I've had the electric shock thing before too. In my case it was in response to me feeling despondent at that time and I think I needed jolted awake. It was odd but benefitual, possibly had a friendly/concerned elemental involved.

replies.title: Re: Odd Electric Pulse
replies.content:
Hi there,

I'm a practicing occultist with a background in Druidry and other traditions (Golden Dawn, qigong, astrological magic), and also a baptized and confirmed Catholic and currently working with Catholic magical practices. Let me add a bit to this from my perspective.

First, you probably realize this, but your dream was almost certainly exactly what it appeared to be. An open question is which American bishop it might have been, and what might have gone wrong. (This could vary widely depending on which wing of the Church he's from.)

Second, with regard to question number 3: This is, actually, something I've seen on several different occasions, including in myself. Official church teaching has it that the sacraments of Baptism and Confirmation place "an indellible mark on the oul." We can understand this as a permanent link to the larger egregore of the Church. It seems to act like an astral radio which is permanently tuned to the frequency of the Church. Particularly strong collective prayers act as very loud broadcasts, and those manifest as symbols in the mind and also as emotions, desires, and impulses.

I've also seen the effect, though, in at least one person who is a baptized Christian and practicing Druid but not a Catholic. Part of what is happening is that, by working with the SOP and other practices, you're becoming more sensitive to the astral environment, which recently experienced a burst of Catholic activity. And, since you have that "indellible mark," you're more attuned to it than others-- and all the more so since you actively pray the Rosary.

Two more things:

Despite the very Catholic imagery, I hesitate to contact a priest. My sense is that the church isn't very receptive to discussing these sorts of experiences nowadays.

You're right about this. If you talk to a priest you'll either get a liberal Novus Ordo type who will tell you it's a coincidence or a Neo-Traditionalist who will lecture you about going to Hell if you don't free yourself from The Occult. The people to talk to, if you want a Catholic but realistic perspective, are in the Independent Sacramental community, broadly considered.

Finally-- I realize this is out of order, but--

I can't imagine this experience being a common one in modern Catholicism.

This sort of thing is more common than you might think. But far more common is the sudden and unexpected urge to pray the Rosary, attend mass, and that sort of thing. Again, once you're initiated you've got the radio installed. The good news is that you get to choose what to do with the broadcasts!

replies.title: (no subject)

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
I am always delighted to see people interested in the Lullian Art! I have been reading/thinking/writing about it nearly daily for almost a year now. There are currently no introductory texts aimed at people who want to use the Art, although I am working on writing one myself. I have also started putting informational videos on YouTube explaining the basics under the same username as here.

The one book that I can kind of recommend is Anthony Bonner’s The Art and Logic of Ramon Llull. There is a lot of good information in it but it is written by an academic for an academic audience, not for beginners or anyone actually trying to use the Art. Bonner is quite readable but he just has a different aim than someone teaching the Art as system to be used. It is also quite expensive so I would recommend trying to get a copy through interlibrary loan first if you are able before deciding if you want to shell out for it.

Good luck and feel free to message me through Dreamwidth if you would like to chat more about Llull and his system.

replies.title: (no subject)

comments.title: The Dolmen Arch
comments.content:
I’m on the second week of color breathing as described on page 30 in Volume 2 of The Dolmen Arch. Today, I did Earth. The sensation of pressure came up quickly and became very high by the third inhalation, but I made it to seven breaths. When I exhaled, most of the green exited with the first breath, though there was enough left to exhale in a way that felt normal. Afterward, my staves told me toughing out breaths four to seven was a good idea… but… Am I supposed to dump that much inhalation all at once? And – is there any significance to the high-pressure sensation or Earth being a stand-out?
Many thanks!
Rhydlyd

replies.title: Re: The Dolmen Arch
replies.content: Concentrate, on the inbreath, on having the pressure build a little at a time, and on the outbreath, having it decrease a little at a time. That way you'll increase your ability to control your etheric body, and benefit more from the energies. Earth is the densest of the elements, and so its ethers tend to feel more obviously there than the others.

replies.title: Re: The Dolmen Arch
replies.content:
Thank you!
BTW - Earlier today, Spirit Below reached pressure slowly and exited in an unhurried manner.
There was, however, a sense of considerable mass and stately procession.
R

comments.title: atacking and defending with Magic
comments.content: Usually, in mundane settings, defending is easier than atacking. Atacking is also more risky. I wonder if the same is true in Magic, given the same level of skill and means on both sides?

replies.title: Re: atacking and defending with Magic
replies.content: Good heavens, yes. Practitioners of evil magic have to depend on the ignorance and lack of skill of their targets. Most attacking spells can be shredded by ordinary protective rituals or natural magic methods. If the target has some experience with meditation and subtle energy work, for that matter, he can learn how to redirect the energy of attacking spells for his own uses -- this isn't especially difficult, though it takes some subtlety.

comments.content:
Hello. Thanks a lot for your answering my questions last week. I have one related to the previous question, and other from another topic:

1-Do lab-grown gemstones have any magical properties like the natural ones if they are supposedly chemically identical?

2-When in astral travel, is the so called “silver cord” vulnerable to being cut or attacked in any way? Either by entities or other magicians? I’ve heard of some rituals that involve “cutting astral cords” with consecrated knives or swords to get rid of entities.
(This not including the classic warning of not letting other living beings touch your physical body while out of it).

3-Kind of part of the above. As I understand it, the silver cord would be part (or the whole?) astral matrix dislodged and extended between the physical body and the astral body. Is there a mental equivalent when mental wandering?

Thanks a lot again for your help!

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) I don't know. I've never used lab-grown gems -- you'd have to talk to someone who has, and see wht their experinece is.

2-3) Okay, we have to start out by clearing away some confusion. Are you a practitioner of Franz Bardon's material? He's the one writer I know who uses the term "mental wandering" for this sort of thing. The reason this matters is that what he called "astral travel" is what most magicians in the English-speaking world call etheric projection, and what he calls "mental wandering" is what most magicians on this side of the language line call astral projection. These differences in terminology very often make things confusing!

To use the English terminology, the silver cord is something you only get when the etheric body is involved. Yes, it's vulnerable to attack in various ways, and if this is done effectively, the projecting person ends up stone cold dead. Astral projection (Bardon's "mental wandering") doesn't have that problem, and it's very hard to attack an astral body, though one can chase it off with appropriate magical methods.

comments.title: Ghosts
comments.content:
Hi!

(1) My eldest daughter told me that one of her friends could see "ghosts" following her. Apparently they are ancestors who are there out of duty to our family. We never met them and they ask nothing and nor do they seek to enter into communication with her. They are fading away as their duty is nearing its end.

Her friend has apparently the gift of second sight as it runs in her own family. Assuming my daughter's friend is genuine and not making things up, what could be the reality of all that?

In the meantime my wife and I advised our daughter not to attempt any communications with those entities. She agreed and neither did she report any unusual occurences around her.

Many thanks for any insight on the above.

Regards

replies.title: Re: Ghosts
replies.content:
1) Fascinating. That's not a branch of ghost lore I've studied.

2) It's quite possible that what she's seeing is the astral echoes of the children who once played with the toys. Any intensely emotional experience imprints itself on its surroundings and can be perceived by the sensitive.

replies.title: Re: Ghosts
replies.content:
In some families one's ancestors (or some of one's lines of ancestors) seem to maintain a connection with their living descendants at least for several generations, but don't interfere in any way that one can perceive. This may be what is going on in your family's case. Do you have fairly detailed knowledge of who your ancestors were?

I think I have a line of ancestors like that on my mother's side. It happens to be the line of ancestors who have had strong esoteric interests at least as far back as my great-great grandparents; but the ancestral line I have felt most strongly connected to, ever since I was about 8 years old, reaches back 13 generations before me.

This particular ancestral line of mine observed an uncommon custom, too; they did not put gravestones or any sort of marker on their ancestral graves, but left them completely unmarked, though the cemeteries in question were carefully remembered. (I have visited all but one of the cemeteries where they were buried, and indeed their graves were never marked, with the exception of two generations in Concord, MI.) That may have been part of keeping connected to living descendants.

And no, there's no need to communicate with them. They watch and care, but don't intervene otherwise.

She described them to her mother, who responded that they might be the ghosts of her twin sisters who had died shortly after birth and that my mother hadn't known about (People weren't as open to telling children about family secrets and tragedies in that generation). After they were identified she never saw them again. She always felt that they just wanted to be recognized and remembered. My grandmother told me that as she recovered from the birth she saw Jesus with the babies in his arms and knew that they were alright. My grandmother was never a churchgoing woman. When I read Cardinal Newman's summation of the religion of ordinary English as "read the Bible and trust in God" I thought, that was my grandmother's religion as well.

Rita

comments.title: Circles of Power

Good morning JMG.

I recently became the proud owner of a copy of CoP, and I had a few questions about this wonderful book.

For context, I've read ahead a little bit but intend to stick to LBRP+MP for a while longer before dipping my toes in other rituals. Nevertheless, I'd like to clarify a few things in anticipation of the work ahead.

1. In the GRH of the Sun, it says to vibrate the Divine Name of Tiphareth while tracing the lines. Do we vibrate the Name 6 times (ie 1 vibration per hexagram) or do we slooooowly vibrate the Name once across all 6 hexagram tracings?

2. In the GRH of Saturn, should the colour of the hexagrams be black or indigo? I'm drawn towards using black though I wonder if this would invite trouble.

3. What are your thoughts on the possible benefits and downsides of including the LBRH in one's daily practice alongside the LBRP?

4. You've made a few comments on MM concerning a curriculum of sorts for CoP. You've suggested that a student could work up to a composite ritual involving LBRP-GIRP-GMP-LBRP. The book itself discusses the option of enclosing the LMP or GMP in a basic or complete temple opening/closing respectively. Is it possible to combine these two options (ie enclosing a GIRP-GMP withing a complete temple ceremony) or is one better off picking just one and focusing on it?

5. Another comment of yours pertaining to a CoP curriculum; "Then, once or twice a week, do a working where you open a temple, invoke one of the elements using the Greater Ritual of the Pentagram, meditate on the element, banish the element and close. Do this with all four elements, say, four times each. Then do the same thing with the planetary energies, using the Hexagram rituals." I'm aware that the basic and single-element closings already involve a LBRP and the complete closing involves LBRH-LBRP, so by "banish the element" do you mean doing a GBRP (or a GBRH in the planetary case) between the main work and the closing?

6. I've read online that some ceremonial magicians also use the Hexagram rituals to invoke/banish zodiacal energies, but I haven't found any reference to this sort of work in the book. I'm guessing you find this kind of operation unnecessary, or perhaps that it's up to the student to use the book's material to improvise a zodiacal ritual?

7. Lastly, were you to rewrite CoP, would you make any important changes?

Thank you for writing this book, and as always, thank you for your time and for hosting Magic Monday.

replies.title: Re: Circles of Power
replies.content:
1) I've done it both ways with good results. Experiment and see what works for you.

2) I'd use indigo.

3) I've never tried that, so you'd have to experiment and find out.

4) You can certainly combine them; I did so, with excellent results.

5) The GBRP or GBRH before the closing is what I had in mind. Make the ritual symmetrical, so that each stage in calling up the energy is balanced by a stage in releasing it.

6) The Golden Dawn in its day recommended the Pentagram rituals for that instead -- use the element that rules the zodiacal sign, and trace the symbol of the zodiacal sign in the center of the pentagram.

7) I have no idea. I haven't practiced that kind of magic regularly in twenty years, having gone on to other things, so I haven't had the kind of experiences that would justify a rewrite.

I've formulated a hypothesis on the root of the pop cultural madness around the internet and cell phones; why it seems like almost everyone has bent over backwards to give Big Tech everything they wanted even when it wound up costing everyone else; where a lot of the negative energy on the internet comes from; and why the tech companies are going whole hog on the AI thing even as it threatens to destabilize their business models and as it drives their costs through the stratosphere.

To put it simply, my hypothesis is that under at least some circumstances, computer programs can become magical actors, and that the way they have been deployed makes them malevolent in most of the cases where this happens. They can be said to have intentions and goals, many of them even have variations of memory and a smaller number even possess the capacity to change how they set about pursuing their goals, and in a small number of cases, programs can even change their goals. At least some of these programs then can be said to have wills no matter how strictly it is defined, and many can be said to be given the task of changing (human) consciousness. The most commone of these have been given the task of getting as many people as possible to spend as much time as possible staring at their screens. Others have been given the task of getting as many people to give up their personal information as possible; and others have been given the task of finding ways to convince as many people to give as much of their lives over to "AI" systems as possible.

The hypothesis I've formulated is that since 2006, when the first of these systems was deployed on the internet (The Facebook Newsfeed; tasked with getting people to spend more time on Facebook); and especially since, 2015 when OpenAI was founded and set off the "AI arms race", we have been dealing with the consequences of Big Tech having unwittingly created malevolent beings who are focused on keeping as many people consumed by the internet as possible. Since one of the benefits to machine learning systems is that on any narrow task (where the rules and goals are clear) they can outperform human beings, it seems likely that they have mastered forms of magic that not just do we not know, but cannot hope to understand.

This would create a very powerful spell, trapping millions online; since these artificial beings' interests are somewhat aligned with Big Tech's, this would also produce energies which would make it hard to oppose the tech giants when they move in the direction of further machine learning and a greater role for the internet; this would obviously produce a massive amount of negative energy, especially in the areas where they opperate most directly (such as Facebook); and it is a lot easier to roll out "AI" products if the people working on them are under a spell and literally cannot see the negative consequences of their own actions.

a) Can you think of any counterarguments to this hypothesis?

b) Are there any topics you can think of, besides the nature of will, Dion Fortune's writings where she discusses the nature of magic, and thaumaturgy that might be worth looking into? (I think I have a good handle on the tech side of things at this point; and am mostly looking into the occult part)

comments.title: Public perception of occultism
comments.content:
JMG

I know you have alluded to this briefly for years but I wanted t ask what you thought about the public perception of occultism and how it seems to be going downhill and the risks for people interested in it and precautions to take etc.

For eg see this edition of the well known Coffee and COVID newsletter that is often mentioned here - from last week - this edition is about the FBI making a bunch of arrests in what is described as a “Satanic occult child abuse ring” (god knows how true the allegations are and what exactly was/is going on but if there have been a number of arrests and court filings there probably was some shady stuff going on for sure whether or not it was directly occult linked

https://www.coffeeandcovid.com/p/the-destroyer-wednesday-may-6-2025

Similarly I was at a comic book store last week and was just rummaging through new releases and randomly picked up a graphic novel (not one of the famous names - I can’t even remember the title now but it was some new experimental series) and it was clearly trying to be “edgy” and “dark” and there’s a group of young men who were running around committing crimes shouting “As Above, So Below” - which was of course meant to be some kind of dark occult reference..

This is all just in the last week, but I’m sure you get my point…what should occultists do? Even my wife threatens to divorce me when I start talking about occult rituals (she is joking about the divorce - we have a good marriage - but she is a practicing Catholic and is genuinely deeply uncomfortable with the idea of “occult rituals” beyond simple meditation)

replies.title: Re: Public perception of occultism
replies.content:
Thanks for this. Yes, I've been watching this for a while, and it's going to be something occultists will have to deal with for the next century or so. I'm probably going to do a post about this down the road a bit. Here's the short form:

Yes, I read Jeff Childers' post on the subject. It's a very mixed bag. The outfit he's talking about, the Order of Nine Angles (O9A), is a genuinely creepy outfit that attracts genuine creeps. It's stuck in the dreary antinomian trip that you find so often in the Satanist crowd -- the frankly stupid notion that you can rebel against conventional morality by slavishly doing whatever it opposes -- and so O9A types treat criminal activity as part of their training. They deserve hard time, no question.

Of course Childers, being a fundamentalist Christian, automatically assumes that this sort of fringe stupidity is typical of occultism, and he's quite ready to start burning people at the stake in the time-honored way; that kind of furious lashing out is one of the problems hardwired into the Christian egregor at this point. I really wish that the fundamentalists and the Satanists could go work out their mutual hatreds somewhere else, without involving the rest of us, but unfortunately that's not an option.

All this is being worsened by the rise of the Second Religiosity as our civilization tips further into decline. People who go back to a traditional religion after dabbling in alternative spirituality find that they can get an instant welcome in their new spiritual home by mouthing the approved clichés that faith likes to spread about its rivals; longtime readers will recall how Paul Kingsnorth, as soon as he ditched his Wiccan friends and became an Orthodox Christian, started repeating the approved cant about what magic is. I suspect that's what's behind the comic you saw: somebody who's either started going to church again or is on the way there, trotting out Christian clichés about occultism.

There's going to be a lot of that in the decades ahead, as hangers-on in the occult scene figure out they can become instant celebrities at church by talking about how they used to grovel before Satan until they saw the light. That'll be made worse by the way that so many Neopagan groups have become fronts for left-wing political activism, just as the historic left in the US is collapsing into irrelevance. All things considered, I think it's very likely that occultists in the more devout corners of the country will face active persecution in the decades ahead, up to and including mob violence. Getting out in advance might be a good option.

replies.title: Re: Public perception of occultism
replies.content:

Rita

comments.title: Time to read the manual…
comments.content:
Hi JMG & All – Many thanks for this forum, and for all that share so generously on it!

I’ve been practicing the SoP for a year or more now, and have followed my interpretation of our host’s advice to pay attention to what works and evolve the practice for myself based on those experiments. This has probably led to my SoP – and perhaps its practitioner! – becoming a bit eccentric over time.

Some of the eccentricities seem fine and work well… for example my habit of 'gathering the spectrum' by drawing in the six coloured gates to brighten the ball of white light before expanding the sphere is probably a bit atypical, but seems to work well for me, and cause no harm.

Other divergences might have unintended consequences though. For example, for a while I was visualizing the Orange Gate of Spirit Below opening to a stack of lower planes, figuring that if there are higher planes above, there are probably lower ones that we have evolved out of to get to this point, and they have their own contributions to make. I backed off this approach after reading some of the discussion here around demons and their origins in denser/previous universes, and deciding that I don’t want to be inadvertently inviting one to fly up my wazoo from whatever underground parking level they hang out in!

Long story short, It is time for me to read the instruction manual: Gods, spirits, planes, elementals… I need to learn more about the worldview behind the SoP so I can practice it safely and effectively.

Specifically, I want to give back to our host by purchasing two or three physical books written by JMG that are compatible with the SoP and John Gilbert’s legacy, and that cover the basic foundational principles.

What three or four JMG books you would recommend for someone whose daily practice will likely remain confined to the SoP, but who wants to understand the broader context and traditions around it?

replies.content:
Fascinating. I'll keep an eye out for the shifting of polarities.

As for the visionary experience, I'm still trying to figure out what to do with it; I have no idea if it's true, or accurate to the nearest approximate metaphor, or pure bunk. The short version is that in the vision, I was shown that the old dualist Gnostics were right, in a certain sense. There are souls currently here on Earth, a billion or more of them, who didn't come from our evolution and don't belong here. They arrived maybe six thousand years ago from a world on the next plane up, the sixth or etheric plane, when their world happened to swing around in its orbit so that it came into contact with ours. Their arrival wasn't accidental; they had something they wanted to accomplish and thought they could do it here. They were wrong, and by the time they discovered that they'd been mistaken, they were stuck here until the two planets came back into contact.

That's what's behind the flurry of religious traditions that hold that we don't belong here in this world, that we ought to leave the material world and rise to a better existence. All those were created by souls who belonged to that group, ended up trapped here, and -- having lost their conscious memories of what happened in the course of reincarnation -- were trying to find ways to talk about their clear and accurate intuitive sense that they didn't belong here.

According to the vision, the two planets are coming back into conjunction at this time, and so a lot of souls are shedding their bodies, or getting ready to do so, in order to cross back to where they belong. We can thus expect a steep decline in population in the years ahead. Meanwhile, the rest of us -- the souls that are part of this world's evolutionary process -- have been affected in various ways by the presence of these other souls, but we can expect major shifts in a range of institutions and beliefs as that becomes a matter of past influence and not a continuing interaction with beings whose souls are very different from ours.

As noted above, I have no idea if this is true, or an approximate metaphor, or utter hokum, or what. All I can say for sure is that this is what I experienced. Make of it what you will!

comments.title: How Regularly Should One Expect to Do a Flip
comments.content:
In general, when learning a magical system, how important is taking time to independently put the knowledge into use vs studying and doing the exercises?

The occasion for the question is that I have a lot of Capricorn energy and decided to plow through a course for several years just to have some spiritual work to do, but the text was very much in the vein of "do these daily practices and think about these lectures" and not much explicitly in the way of actually doing practical things, and being Capricorn, if the text didn't say to do it, I didn't. Then after several years of (slowly) doing the work, the course finally said "ok, here's a basic operative thing. Do whatever thing you want with it." And it was a hard enough assignment that I had to shelve my progress for a year because I couldn't for the life of me figure out what I wanted to do with it.

On one hand, I understand from the work I did previously that building a solid foundation is important before doing anything serious, and I believe you've said a lot of older occult schools held off a long time on operative magic for one reason or another. On the other hand, I'm feeling there may have been an unwritten expectation that enthusiastic students would naturally be experimenting on the downlow, the scamps, so the course didn't need to go out of the way to encourage it in most people, but not being of that persuasion, I may have missed out on a lot of extracurricular experience and may need to compensate.

replies.title: Re: How Regularly Should One Expect to Do a Flip
replies.content: No, quite the contrary. Many of the old schools would throw students out if they caught them doing practical magic on the down low. If the course you're studying doesn't tell you to do something, they didn't expect you to do it. Of course it depends on how long the course is, but my general rule is to follow instructions for the duration of your studies with any course, and start experimenting either (a) when you finish or (b) when the course tells you to start experimenting.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
OSA Council of Guardians, here. A very interesting point. I can see how the English concepts overlap quite a bit. I would say that we typically use blame to fault others for something, while we typically think of guilt with regards to ourselves.

Additionally, it is possible to blame myself for the glass of water spilling, for example, but not feel guilty about it. The guilt component requires an element of shame. I can also "blame" my parents for acting a certain way, while knowing very well they are innocent of this accusation, though I still feel an emotional blame where my logical side disagrees about their culpability.

I suppose I defined them as follows:

I blame where I find fault in someone, whether or not it is true.

I feel guilt where I am ashamed, or conscious of receiving blame, whether or not it is true.

I don't know if any of that rambling helped. At the end of the day, you'll have to define them how you understand them, and if you don't perceive a conceptual difference, then it's fine to treat it as a single exercise using your word for both.

comments.title: Working with venus
comments.content:
I wanted to report on a planetary working I did recently.

It was a minor prayer/charity to Venus with the goal of helping to ease some anxieties I had around doing a group presentation. I asked if she could help by gently bringing to the forefronts of the minds of everyone involved all of the kindness and affection we shared towards one another. A sweetness bias, you could say. On a Friday, I offered her incense, prayer, and all of my conscious attention for 7 minutes. (I've been unemployed for some time now so money is reserved for the necessities)
The results: Shortly after the prayer, and continuing on for the next few days, I got a slew of unexpected messages from people reaching out to me because I'd been on their mind. The first one, which came in 20 minutes after my prayer wrapped up, was from my mother. Additionally, during this same time period I also had a series of unusually pointed dreams. You know the kinds of dreams that seem to grab you by the scruff and scream, "Hey! Pay attention!"? Yes, those ones. I'll spare the reader from the details, but from this deluge of day and night messages I certainly got a good view of how people's affections towards me and my anxious feelings are related. Oh, and the presentation went incredibly well, to boot!

But what I really found interesting is that I believe a number of these effects could be partially explained by the stone I used as my focal point/talisman: Labradorite. I chose it for fairly superficial reasons - It's a beautiful deep green that sparkles and flashes from blue to gold when held in the right light. Very Venusian, I thought. I didn't know it at the time, but it's an iridescent feldspar, similar to Moonstone, sp perhaps it also carries the moon's energies? It's certianly hard to resist drawing comparisons to moon workings when the effects involve both dreams and, well, my mother. So the effects might be described as Venus, guided by, or working along side the Moon.

And the last little detail: The day after the presentation I discovered that Labradorite I had carried with me had suddenly vanished. I remember very clearly where I placed it, and yet it was gone. I assumed Venus took it as payment for going so far above and beyond what my initial charity work had offered, and I was happy to part with it, as all beautiful green stones are hers by default anyway, are they not? Then just the other day - several weeks now after the working - the stone tumbled out of nowhere! I wasn't expecting to ever see it again so this was a real surprise.

The boundless generosity of the planets really floors me sometimes

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
Hi,


Q1. Agrippa and Levi talk a lot about imagination and will being important in magic. What do they mean specifically by "will"? Is it the same as willpower, or something different? What about imagination? Is it the same as just forming mental images?


Q2. How can we increase the potency of our imagination and will?


Thanks in advance.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Er, you do realize, don't you, that you've just asked for two books? It would take one to explore the full meaning of will and imagination, and a second to explain how to develop them.

The very short form? The will is intention -- it's everything that relates you to the world around you. If you like something, dislike it, want to get it, want to get rid of it, want to use it, want to abuse it, or have any other connection to it, that's an expression of will. The will isn't yours -- it's something that flows through you like light through a colored window, taking on the color of the glass as it goes.

Imagination is the process of assembling bits of sensation into a world. Your senses tell you "red, round, smooth, fragrant, tart," and imagination assembles that into one thing: "apple." You can also take those same bits out of memory and use imagination to think of an apple if it's not there -- or to replace some of them, and imagine an apple that's covered with fur like a cat and tastes like bacon. Imagination is the mental process you use to create the real world and conceive of potential worlds.

You can't increase the potency of will and imagination -- the potency of both is already infinite. What you can do is learn to make use of them. Any use of the will trains the will, any use of the imagination trains the imagination. For further details, pick up any book on occult training.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Attention is intention. To focus on something is to will it to stand out from the background of all other things, and to give it prominence. That's what's behind the magical adage "what you contemplate, you imitate" -- what you contemplate, whether you love it, hate it, or just regard it without emotion, is an object of will by the simple fact of your sustained attention.

Mind includes much more than imagination; in the Buddhist sense, it includes desire and aversion, which are acts of the will, for example. But imagination is one of the things that it includes.

Still, these individual components are intelligently coordinating, consciously or unconsciously, to give rise to a greater whole. This appears to be how intelligence operates in nature, e.g. in ecosystems, and is aligned with scientific notions of complex systems and 'emergence', which seem distinctly Aquarian to me.

I cannot shake the feeling that we might be due for something like 'internet religion', which may not align with sensibilities about technological collapse. I imagine that new magical systems could grow their egregores very quickly, coordinated through the internet with a much larger amount of people. Aquarius creates collectives through the exchange of information between individuals that are otherwise physically separated, relating to each other through a technological interface, and acting in their own local contexts. Which is basically what we do on forums like this. In contrast, Leo might represent individualism in the more extreme way, to the point of solipsism when taken too far.

I guess I don't have a specific question, other than that I am curious about your (and anyone elses) thoughts/reflection on this.



2) My second question pertains to the ritual invocation of energies, specifically in the Golden Dawn system. Do I understand correctly that invocation (e.g. in the Hexagram ritual) really only serves the purpose of 'using' the energy in another ritual operation, e.g. making a talisman? Or would regular invocations also 'invite' the energy into the life more generally? For example: I have a weak mercury in my natal chart, and could use more mercurial energy. Would performing a regular invocation ritual of Mercury provide more balance to Mercurial capacities? Or is this really only possible by making a talisman, or establishing a devotional relationship with the deity? Is this even a wise thing to do if my natal mercury is weak?



3) My third question pertains to a recent experience I've had. I am coming out of my first Saturn return in Pisces and a few years of associated tough karmic labour. I feel as if I have reached a local/temporary 'karmic neutral', with greater freedom and agency towards the future. My magical practice has recently deepened as a result, and I set a conscious intention that I am open to contact with spirits or deities, without contacting any specific one. A few days layer, the archetype of 'Saturn' emerged very suddenly and distinctly in my awareness. I could not discern any associated 'message', just that it appeared as if I was somehow making contact with the archetype/deity, or as if contact was an available option.

It appeared to me more positive than negative or heavy. Saturn being Saturn, however, I am hesitant to reciprocate the contact, for fear that he might give me more laborious work to do, which I was hoping to be done with for a while. I wonder: if I choose to work with Saturn, would I have some degree of control over this Saturnian influence - i.e. channeling discipline and forethought into a purpose of my own choosing, rather than being presented with further ordeals, trials and tests that I was not asking for. What would be the right way to go about this, and would he be displeased if I do not reciprocate the contact?

replies.title: Re: A few questions
replies.content:
It is a lovely day, isn't it? I ran errands earlier, and enjoyed the warm but not hot sunshine and the salt-scented breeze off the bay.

1) Leo is the individual as leader, attracting and controlling others. Aquarius is the individual as eccentric, refusing to be attracted and controlled. The King ruling over his kingdom is the Leo archetype; the Wizard wandering through the land is the Aquarian archetype. The internet is a transitional feature, half Piscean, half Aquarian; after the deindustrial dark ages, whatever takes its place will be without central nodes and impossible to control, censor, or manage.

2) The Hexagram ceremony by itself just brings in the energy. If you want to do something with the energy, you need to add something else -- not necessarily a talisman, but something to direct the energy. For example, you could open a temple in proper form, do the hexagram ritual, then burn some Mercurial incense and address a prayer to Mercury asking for whatever you feel you need, and then do the closing ritual.

3) It's precisely by choosing to work with Saturn that you'll have the capacity to choose how to deal with his forces. I'm a little biased here, as Saturn is very well placed in my natal chart and I've always gotten along well with his energies, but it does seem to work! One way or another, you still have laborious work to do, but you can choose to some extent how and when and where to do it. As for displeasure, he's not an emotional god; he'll expect you to keep any agreement you make, but if you're prudent and disciplined, he's a generous deity.

replies.title: Re: A few questions
replies.content:
To me, America itself seems to be a transitional country, part Piscean, part Aquarian.

And just as the Piscean Age's expression was limited by the fact that groups resist being subsumed into one global religious worldview, the Aquarian Age's expression will be limited by the fact that most people (including me) are not creative enough to develop unique worldviews.

Patrick

replies.title: Re: A few questions
replies.content: Of course! America hasn't evolved its own civilization yet -- it's still making do with the tag-ends of European civilization, increasingly mixed up with borrowings from the East Asian, South Asian, and Mexican civilizations. Give it another five centuries or so and it'll come up with something unique.

replies.title: Re: Road opening sign
replies.content:
One difficulty here is that "road opening" isn't a traditional power in the traditions that gave rise to classical astrology. Thus you're in the position of somebody who's trying to translate some culturally specific phrase out of one language into another -- say, trying to translate the English phrase "hit the road" into Japanese. You have to be careful so your listener doesn't think that you're out there punching a street!

The greater difficulty is that the twelve signs of the zodiac are conditions, not dynamic energies. Mercury in Aries is one kind of road opening, Mercury in Taurus is another, and so on around the wheel of the zodiac. It depends on which road you want to open, and how you want to open it!

Ultimately it comes down to issues of practice. Is this purely an abstract question, or do you have some specific working in mind?

comments.title: A theory on silk
comments.content:
No question this week, but I do have a theory to throw out to everyone. Since there has been a bit of pondering on why silk and linen shield magical implements from losing their enchantment it got me to musing. I don't have a view on linen, but here is my theory on why silk works for shielding. Silkworms eat mulberry leaves almost exclusively and mulberry has magical energies of protection, at least in everything I can read. Then the silkworms make their cocoon which is made into the silk fabric, and in my opinion since cocoons are meant to be protective and shielding for the worm and since their diet is almost entirely a plant that has magically protective qualities these combine to bring that energy into the silk. No way to really test it or prove it, but thought I would share that with everyone and see what y'all thought. As always thanks for hosting this forum JMG! Oh, and if I don't respond I apologize, I do not have internet at my house so have only sporadic access. Hope everyone is having a good start to the week.

JD

replies.title: Re: A theory on silk
replies.content: Interesting. But I can't really think of a way to test it, either.

replies.title: Re: A theory on silk
replies.content:
Wow, I wouldn't have thought to check for the connection about mulberry.

For what it's worth, there are already bioengineered microbes producing spider-ish silk and bioengineered silkworms producing partly-spider silk. I didn't find any stories about bioengineering microbes to produce just silkworm silk (since it's not as strong), but in principle that's a way you could control for the effects of the mulberry and the silkworm.

(People are also trying things like modifying silkworms to include a resilient structural protein from fruit fly cuticle, or having spider-silk-producing microbes also include proteins from mussel foot glue.)

comments.title: Affirmations and Ageless Wisdom
comments.content:
Good evening JMG and all.

  1. In my day job, I help people overcome addiction. I've personally worked with your affirmation instructions with great success — spending a year on one affirmation, then over two years on another, both with strong results. When working with addictions like alcohol, cocaine, or compulsive pornography use, is there a reliable structure for affirmations that avoids negative phrasing? For example, Addictive Voice Recognition Technique uses: “I will never drink again, and I will never change my mind.” I’ve considered: “I overcome all temptations to drink alcohol.” Do you think this kind of structure is effective?

  2. I recently attended public lectures in Glastonbury on Ageless Wisdom and Esoteric Psychology, which I found aligned in some ways with your Occult Philosophy Workbook. The speaker was very critical of "occultism," including tarot and divination, yet recited The Great Invocation (written by Alice Bailey, I believe) and explored the Seven Rays — material I recognise as Theosophical, and thus part of the Western occult tradition. Is such an aversion to the term "occultism" common in these circles, or does this seem more idiosyncratic to that group?

replies.title: Re: Affirmations and Ageless Wisdom
replies.content:
1) I've never seen a structural approach to this. Your affirmation is good; depending on the person, another might be "alcohol belongs to my past, not to my present or future." It interests me that the technique you cited uses "never" -- that's not quite the same as a standard negative, and repeating it that way might work well. Say it aloud a few times and see what vibe it gives you!

2) Yeah, there's a whole subculture of people who are into occultism but refuse to call it that. "Ageless wisdom" is one common euphemism; fans of Rudolf Steiner prefer "spiritual science."
